text.title: The rebuild test as acceptance gate
text.summary: The rebuild test is the project-spec's acceptance criterion. It runs at well-defined cadence points, not on a calendar.

rule.statement: project-spec/ + ui-ux-design.md + constitution.md MUST contain enough information that a mid-level dev (unfamiliar with the codebase) can rebuild the project in two weeks.
rule.rationale: The two-week ceiling forces concrete contracts and removes "we just know how it works" tribal knowledge. Mid-level is the calibration target because senior devs paper over gaps with experience.

rule.statement: Run the rebuild test at end-of-feature, immediately before moving the feature folder to done/.
rule.rationale: The feature is the unit that mutates project-spec. Catching gaps at the boundary is the cheapest fix point.

rule.statement: Run the rebuild test before each deploy that follows a CR or BUG fix.
rule.rationale: CR/BUG changes often patch a missing business rule or undocumented edge — the spec must absorb the lesson before the deploy goes out, not after.

antipattern.description: Scheduling the rebuild test on a quarterly cron.
antipattern.reason: By the time the cron fires the drift is months deep and the team treats it as a chore. Tie the test to delivery events.

section.title: How to run it (LLM dry-run protocol)

list.item: Spin up an agent with read access ONLY to project-spec/, ui-ux-design.md, constitution.md. Strip code-repo access.
Ask: "rebuild the project from these documents. List every assumption you must invent." Capture the assumption list verbatim.
Each assumption that maps to real-code reality is a project-spec gap. File as a CR or fold into the in-flight feature's spec delta.
Pass criterion: the assumption list contains nothing that affects user-visible behaviour, public API shape, or invariants.

rule.statement: Failing the rebuild test BLOCKS feature promotion to done/ and BLOCKS post-CR/BUG deploy.
rule.rationale: Soft enforcement turns the test into a ritual; hard enforcement keeps the spec live.
